chunks.text: Form No: HCJD/C-121 ORDER SHEET IN THE LAHORE HIGH COURT, LAHORE (JUDICIAL DEPARTMENT) Case No. Crl.Misc.26602-M/2022 Mubbashar Farooq Versus Addl. Sessions Judge, etc. Sr.No.of order/ Proceedings Date of order/ Proceedings Order with signatures of Judge, and that of parties or counsel, where necessary. 09.12.2022 Mr. Asif Miran, Advocate for the petitioner. Dr. Muhammad Anwar Khan Gondal, Addl. Prosecutor General with Saif Ullah, SI. ====== Through this petition under Section 561-A Cr.P.C. the petitioner Mubashar Farooq has called in question validity of impugned orders dated 15.02.2022 and 31.03.2022 passed by the learned courts below. Through the former learned Trial Court dismissed his application under Section 249- A Cr.P.C. for pre-mature acquittal in case FIR No.233/18 dated 02.08.2018, in respect of an offence under Section 489-F PPC, registered at P.S. City Wazirabad, District Gujranwala and through the latter learned Revisional Court dismissed his revision against the order of learned Trial Court. 2. Facts necessary for disposal of instant petition are that respondent No.3 /complainant (hereinafter shall be called as complainant) got registered aforementioned case against the petitioner alleging therein that he is a businessman; that petitioner on account of business transaction issued cheque No.10031182 for an amount of Rs.4,78,615/- which was dishonoured on his presentation before the concerned bank. W.P. No.26602-M/2022 2 3. After completion of investigation, challan was submitted in the Court for trial. During pendency of trial, the petitioner filed an application under Section 249-A Cr.P.C. for his pre-mature acquittal, inter-alia, on the grounds that civil suit under Order XXXVII CPC filed by the complainant on the basis of disputed cheque has been returned by the trial court under Order 7 Rule 10 CPC vide judgment dated 17.05.2021 with the observation that the disputed cheque was infact issued as guarantee/security; that the complainant did not challenge the said order before the Appellate Forum as such the same has attained finality; that after these findings there remains no probability of conviction of the petitioner, as such he may be acquitted of the charge. 4.

After completion of investigation, challan was submitted in the Court for trial. During pendency of trial, the petitioner filed an application under Section 249-A Cr.P.C. for his pre-mature acquittal, inter-alia, on the grounds that civil suit under Order XXXVII CPC filed by the complainant on the basis of disputed cheque has been returned by the trial court under Order 7 Rule 10 CPC vide judgment dated 17.05.2021 with the observation that the disputed cheque was infact issued as guarantee/security; that the complainant did not challenge the said order before the Appellate Forum as such the same has attained finality; that after these findings there remains no probability of conviction of the petitioner, as such he may be acquitted of the charge. 4. After hearing pro and contra arguments, learned Trial Court dismissed the said application vide impugned order dated 15.02.2022 and revision against the said order also met the same fate vide impugned order dated 31.03.2022. Hence, this petition. 5. Despite service complainant did not turn up. 6. I have heard the arguments advanced by the learned counsel for the petitioner and learned Law Officer appearing for the State and gone through the record. 7. The petitioner filed application under Section 249-A Cr.P.C. for his pre-mature acquittal. Before proceeding further it is expedient to go through the provisions of said section which reads as under:- “249-A. Power of Magistrate to acquit accused at any stage: Nothing in this Chapter shall be deemed to prevent a Magistrate from acquitting an accused W.P. No.26602-M/2022 3 at any stage of the case if after hearing the Prosecutor and the accused and for reasons to be recorded, he considered that the charge is groundless or there is no probability of the accused being convicted of any offence.” (emphasis supplied). 8. Bare reading of aforesaid provisions of law makes it abundantly clear that the Legislature has empowered the Magistrate to acquit an accused at any stage of the case, if after hearing the Prosecutor and accused it arrived at a definite conclusion that the charge is groundless or there is no probability of conviction of the accused. 9. Keeping in view above two grounds in mind, I have to see as to whether the case of the petitioner falls in anyone of these two grounds for invoking the provisions of Section 249-A Cr.P.C. The allegation against the petitioner was that due to business transaction, he issued disputed cheque for an amount of Rs.4,78,615/- in favour of the complainant, which was dishonoured on its presentation before the concerned bank.

Keeping in view above two grounds in mind, I have to see as to whether the case of the petitioner falls in anyone of these two grounds for invoking the provisions of Section 249-A Cr.P.C. The allegation against the petitioner was that due to business transaction, he issued disputed cheque for an amount of Rs.4,78,615/- in favour of the complainant, which was dishonoured on its presentation before the concerned bank. I have gone through the copy of disputed cheque available on record and observed that on the back of it, it was clearly mentioned that “This cheque is not for payment, only for memorandum of payment written already.” Copy of memorandum of payment mentioned on the back of dishonoured cheque is also available on record, which was infact a decision of arbitrators regarding dispute between Shahid Pervaiz and Muhammad Ishaque. It was specifically mentioned therein that the disputed cheque was issued as “guarantee” to said Shahid Pervaiz. Nowhere in the entire memorandum name of the complainant was find W.P. No.26602-M/2022 4 mentioned even as a witness of decision and apparently he in connivance with said Shahid Pervaiz misused the disputed cheque in order to rope the petitioner in a case of criminal liability. Now the question arises as to whether the cheque which was not issued for the purpose of encashment constituted an offence under Section 489-F PPC or not. In order to determine this question, we have to go through the provisions of Section 489-F of PPC, which for ready reference are reproduced as under:- 489-F. Dishonestly issuing a cheque.—Whoever dishonestly issues a cheque towards payment of a loan or fulfillment of an obligation which is dishonoured on presentation, shall be punished with imprisonment which may extend to three years or with fine, or with both, unless he can establish, for which the burden of proof shall rest on him, that he had made arrangements with his bank to ensure that the cheque would be honoured and that bank was at fault in not honouring the cheque.” From the above provision of law it is manifestly clear that there are following three pre-requisites for constitution of offence under Section 489-F PPC:- (i) Issuance of cheque with dishonest intention. (ii) Cheque was issued towards payment of loan or fulfillment of an obligation. (iii) Cheque was dishonoured. 10.

In order to determine this question, we have to go through the provisions of Section 489-F of PPC, which for ready reference are reproduced as under:- 489-F. Dishonestly issuing a cheque.—Whoever dishonestly issues a cheque towards payment of a loan or fulfillment of an obligation which is dishonoured on presentation, shall be punished with imprisonment which may extend to three years or with fine, or with both, unless he can establish, for which the burden of proof shall rest on him, that he had made arrangements with his bank to ensure that the cheque would be honoured and that bank was at fault in not honouring the cheque.” From the above provision of law it is manifestly clear that there are following three pre-requisites for constitution of offence under Section 489-F PPC:- (i) Issuance of cheque with dishonest intention. (ii) Cheque was issued towards payment of loan or fulfillment of an obligation. (iii) Cheque was dishonoured. 10. Here in the instant case as has been observed supra, on the back of disputed cheque it has been specifically mentioned that the same has not been issued for the purpose of payment, as such one of the foundational element to attract the provisions of Section 489-F PPC is patently missing in the W.P. No.26602-M/2022 5 instant case. In case reported as “Mian Allah Ditta..Vs.. The State and others (2013 SCMR 51),” it has been held by the Hon’ble Apex Court that:- “ Every transaction where a cheque is dishonoured may not constitute an offence. The foundational elements to constitute an offence under this provision are issuance of a chaeque with dishonest intent, the cheque shuld be towards repayment of a loan or fulfillment of an obligation and lastly that the cheque in question is dishonoured.” 11. Admittedly, on the basis of disputed cheque the complainant also filed a suit for Recovery under Order XXXVII CPC, however, the plaint was returned by the learned Trial Court vide judgment dated 17.05.2021 with the observation that the disputed cheque was issued as “guarantee”. The complainant did not challenge the said judgment before Appellate Forum, as such the same has attained finality. 12. In the attending circumstances, I am of the considered view that provisions of Section 489-F PPC are not attracted in the instant case, as such recording of prosecution evidence would be a futile exercise as even after recording of the same there is no probability of conviction of the petitioner. Both the courts below have failed to comprehend correct proposition of law while passing impugned orders which cannot be allowed to hold field. 13.

In the attending circumstances, I am of the considered view that provisions of Section 489-F PPC are not attracted in the instant case, as such recording of prosecution evidence would be a futile exercise as even after recording of the same there is no probability of conviction of the petitioner. Both the courts below have failed to comprehend correct proposition of law while passing impugned orders which cannot be allowed to hold field. 13. For what has been discussed above, this petition is allowed as a result whereof impugned orders of learned courts below dated 15.02.2022 and 31.03.2022 are set-aside. Resultantly, the petition is acquitted of the charge from the W.P. No.26602-M/2022 6 aforementioned case. He is on bail. His surety is discharged from the liability. (Asjad Javaid Ghural) Judge Approved for Reporting Judge Azam*
